content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


July 11, 1988


Henry S. Barbosa
Barbosa and Vera
Attorneys at Law
Los Angeles Corporate Center
1000 Corporate Center Drive, Suite 350
Monterey Park, CA 91754

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-241

Dear Mr. Barbosa:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding
application of the new restrictions on publicly-funded mass
mailings under Proposition 73.

Based on our telephone conversation and your summary of the
points we discussed regarding the limitations now in effect, I
am confident that you have a good understanding of the current
application of the law. As applies to Montebello Today, you
are correct in stating that this periodical would comply with
the new law when the message is under the signature of a
non-elected city official. You may continue to display, depict,
reference, specifically name and include photographs of
non-elected city officials in the periodical.

In reviewing the copy of Montebello Today which you
forwarded, I have found two articles which include specific
reference to Mayor Arnold Glasman (see page 6 of the
periodical). In addition to the deletion of the photographs
you noted, we would advise that the two references to Mayor
Glasman, and any other references to elected officials be
deleted from the newsletter.

I am enclosing a copy of our July 1, 1988 follow-up letter
regarding interpretation of the mass mailing restrictions. In


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Henry S. Barbosa
July 11, 1988
Page 2


the future, we expect to address additional questions
concerning the mass mailing restrictions in Proposition 73, and
we have included your name on our mailing list for information
on the subject.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: [illegible]
                                            Lilly Spitz
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:LS:plh

Enclosure

BARBOSA & VERA
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

HENRY S. BARBOSA
RONALD T. VERA
RICHARD J. MORILLO
DOUGLAS D. BARNES
JAMES R. HOLLEY
LYNNE S. BASSIS
ANA L. FIGUEROA

LOS ANGELES CORPORATE CENTER
SUITE 350
1000 CORPORATE CENTER DRIVE
MONTEREY PARK, CALIFORNIA 91754
(213) 263-5199

655 SOUTH HOPE STREET
SUITE 1300
LOS ANGELES, CA 90017
(213) 688-7927

OF COUNSEL
MICHAEL S. BERNICK

June 23, 1988

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Your File No.: A-88-220
     Prop. 73

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

     This will evidence my telephone conversation with Ms. Lilly Spitz of the Legal Division relative to questions I have raised on behalf of my client, the City of Montebello.

     In accord with that conversation, I have enclosed a copy of a periodical produced by Montebello Tomorrow, Inc., a nonprofit corporation organized by the City of Montebello, entitled Montebello Today. I have redlined those elements of the Montebello Today periodical which we understand would be objectionable and prohibited under your guidelines.

     Let me summarize my understanding of some points discussed with Ms. Spitz.

     The Montebello Today periodical, and any other informational mailings by the City would comply with both the ballot measure and the FPPC's regulations when the message is under signature or authorized by a non-elected official, such as the City Administrator or some similar officer. In this regard, we would continue to display, depict and photograph non-elected City officials in the Montebello Today.

Robert E. Leidigh
June 23, 1988
Page Two


     Let me close by extending my appreciation to Ms. Spitz for
her gracious and courteous assistance in this matter.  If my
understanding is incorrect in any respect, please advise at
your earliest convenience.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Henry S. Barbosa

HSB:ng 567

Enclosure

cc:  City Administrator,
     City of Montebello

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 27, 1988


Henry S. Barbosa
Barbosa & Vera
Los Angeles Corporate Center
1000 Corporate Center Drive, Suite 350
Monterey Park, CA 91754

Re: 88-241

Dear Mr. Barbosa:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on June 27, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Lilly Spitz, an attorney in the
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: As applies to Montebello Today, you are correct in stating that this periodical would comply with the new law when the message is under the signature of a non-elected city official. You may continue to display, depict, reference, specifically name and include photographs of non-elected city officials in the periodical. However, two articles which include specific reference to Mayor Arnold Glasman must be deleted, and any other references to elected officials should be deleted from the newsletter, in addition to deletion of photographs of elected officials.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the city's Montebello Today periodical complies with Proposition 73 restrictions on publicly-funded mass mailings, specifically regarding references to and depictions of elected officials.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that Montebello Today complies with Proposition 73 when messages are signed by non-elected officials and non-elected officials are depicted. However, all specific references to Mayor Arnold Glasman and other elected officials must be deleted from the newsletter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Multi-part document including incoming request letter (June 23, 1988), FPPC acknowledgment letter (June 27, 1988), and FPPC advice response (July 11, 1988). Primary response is the July 11 letter from M. Griffiths. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; sections extracted from body text. High confidence in extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the city's Montebello Today periodical complies with Proposition 73 restrictions on publicly-funded mass mailings, specifically regarding references to and depictions of elected officials.

CONCLUSION: As applies to Montebello Today, you are correct in stating that this periodical would comply with the new law when the message is under the signature of a non-elected city official. You may continue to display, depict, reference, specifically name and include photographs of non-elected city officials in the periodical. However, two articles which include specific reference to Mayor Arnold Glasman must be deleted, and any other references to elected officials should be deleted from the newsletter, in addition to deletion of photographs of elected officials.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to inquiry about Proposition 73 restrictions on publicly-funded mass mailings, specifically addressing the City of Montebello's Montebello Today periodical and requirements for removal of references to elected officials.
